latest.title: Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 12:36
RNS Number : 8081J
Atlas Metals Group PLC
25 June 2026
25 June 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company, announces will issue further ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") pursuant to the Company's
"at-the-market" facility with Axis Capital Markets Limited ("
Axis
"; the
"
At-the-Market Facility
"
)
.
At-the-Market Facility issue
1,339,180 new Ordinary Shares ("
ATM Shares
") will be issued at an average price of 7.3 pence per ATM Share, in connection with the At-the-Market Facility. As announced by the Company on 5 March 2026 all proceeds from the
At-the-Market Facility will go towards repayment of the funding facility currently in place with YA II PN Ltd, an institutional investor managed by Yorkville Advisors Global, LP.
Admission and total voting rights
Application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of
1,339,180
ATM Shares to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission is expected to occur on or around 26 June 2026.
When issued, the ATM Shares will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 43,889,001
Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 43,889,001. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 16:01
RNS Number : 7604F
Atlas Metals Group PLC
26 May 2026
26 May 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company, announces that it has received a conversion notice from YA II PN Ltd, an institutional investor managed by Yorkville Advisors Global, LP ("
Lender
" or "
Yorkville
") under the convertible loan agreement ("
Funding Facility
") previously announced by the Company on 5 March 2026. Atlas further announces that it will additionally issue further ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") pursuant to the Company's
"at-the-market" facility with Axis Capital Markets Limited ("
Axis
"; the
"
At-the-Market Facility
"
)
.
Funding Facility conversion
The Company has received a conversion notice from the Lender pursuant to the Funding Facility to convert £86,780.82 of the outstanding principal and accrued interest under the second loan of up to £2.0 million, as announced on 5 March 2026, into new Ordinary Shares at a price of 7.80 pence per Ordinary Share. Accordingly, the Lender will be issued with 1,112,574 new Ordinary Shares ("
Conversion Shares
").
At-the-Market Facility issue
In addition to the issue of the Conversion Shares, the Company further announces that 2,255,820 new Ordinary Shares ("
ATM Shares
", together with the Conversion Shares the "
New Shares
") will be issued at an average price of 8.795 pence per ATM Share, in connection with the At-the-Market Facility. As announced by the Company on 5 March 2026 all proceeds from the
At-the-Market Facility will go towards repayment of the Funding Facility.
All Ordinary Shares previously issued pursuant to the At-the-Market Facility have been placed and Axis currently holds no Ordinary Shares.
Chris Chadwick, Chief Executive Officer of Atlas Metals, commented:
"We are grateful to the continued support from Yorkville and the confidence they have demonstrated in Atlas Metals and our strategy through their conversion. The Company remains focused on completing the transformative acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd and I look forward to providing further updates in due course as matters progress."
Admission and total voting rights
Application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 3,368,394 New Shares to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission is expected to occur on or around 27 May 2026.
When issued, the New Shares will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 42,549,821 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 42,549,821. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
For further information, please contact:

company_30s.what_they_do: AMG.L is a small‑cap resource company focused on uranium exploration and development in Central Asia.  Its main asset is a large deposit in Kyrgyzstan that could supply the growing demand for clean‑energy fuels.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is a critical component of nuclear power, which is expanding as countries look to reduce carbon emissions.  A well‑managed deposit in a politically stable country could become a key supply source, giving AMG a strategic niche in a market that is expected to grow steadily over the next decade.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early stages of its project, relying on a mix of equity issuances and credit facilities to fund drilling and permitting.  It is not yet producing revenue and is burning cash as it moves toward first‑stage development.  The share price is currently in a very low range, reflecting the uncertainty and high risk associated with the project.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show a steady stream of capital‑raising activity: new ordinary shares, a £2.5 million funding facility, and a revised auditor appointment.  These moves signal that the company is actively seeking liquidity to keep drilling and permitting on track.

Several updates relate to the Kyrgyz uranium asset – including a new competent persons report and a progress update on the deposit.  This suggests the company is working to secure the technical and regulatory approvals needed to move into production.
The AGM and annual results indicate that the company is still operating at a loss, but the board is focused on maintaining a viable capital structure and aligning with institutional investors.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent but restless – the community is largely quiet, with a handful of bullish voices pointing to the asset’s potential, while a few skeptics question the timeline.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the size of the deposit and the strategic importance of uranium.  A few traders see the company as a long‑term play that could benefit from a clean‑energy boom.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish posts point to the company’s lack of revenue, heavy cash burn, and the fact that it has not yet proven the viability of its mining operations.  Some fear a repeat of the early‑stage failures that plague many resource startups.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has been flat, with no new posts in the last week.  The lack of discussion may signal that the market is ignoring the stock, creating a window for a contrarian move if a catalyst appears.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search activity for AMG.L, confirming that the market is currently oblivious to this name.

near_term_catalysts: A formal feasibility study or a signed resource estimate could provide the technical validation needed for investors to commit capital.
An announcement of a partnership or joint venture with a larger mining group would signal that the project has moved beyond the exploration stage.
The completion of a key permitting milestone in Kyrgyzstan could unlock access to the deposit, creating a clear trigger for a price rally.
A scheduled press release on the company’s funding facility could bring in fresh equity, improving the balance sheet and reducing the risk of a liquidity crunch.
expect_next.watch_for: Key indicators over the next 2–3 months include: 1) a spike in trading volume that exceeds 10 × the 20‑day average, 2) a clear upward break of the 50‑day moving average on the daily chart, 3) a positive comment from a senior mining analyst or a mention of the company in a sector‑wide research report, and 4) any announcement that the Kyrgyz deposit has been certified as commercially viable.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep crash cycle – the price has dropped dramatically from its peak, and the lack of trading activity suggests that capitulation may already be complete.  The APEX score indicates a tight price compression, which is a classic sign that a rebound could be imminent if a catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company can secure a feasibility report or a partnership within the next quarter, the stock could recover to the middle of its 52‑week range.  Given the current depth of the crash, this would represent a 70–90 % upside.  The historical performance of deep‑crash signals (average return 39 % for GREEN, 112 % for ORANGE) suggests that a single bounce could more than compensate for the short‑term volatility.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest downside is that the company has not yet proven that the uranium deposit is economically recoverable.  If the feasibility study comes back negative, the price could fall further into the lower 52‑week range, potentially wiping out the remaining equity holders.  A liquidity crunch could force a forced sale of shares at a steep discount.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: The worst‑case loss is the current market value (essentially a 100 % loss of invested capital).  Reward: A 70–90 % upside if the price rebounds to the mid‑range.  The asymmetry is roughly 1:1.5 – a single win could recover the loss and deliver a modest profit.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter a small position (e.g., 1–2 % of your portfolio) if you see a volume spike of 10× the 20‑day average coupled with a break above the 50‑day moving average.  Use a tight stop 5 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout, and consider a trailing stop once the price reaches 30 % above the entry.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a cash position until you see any of the following: a positive feasibility report, a partnership announcement, or a significant increase in daily volume (≥10 × the 20‑day average).  Once any of these triggers occur, consider a quick entry with a small position size.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for insolvency, a forced liquidation of shares, or a delisting notice within the next 6 months, exit immediately.  No other terminal risks are currently evident.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AMG.L is a deep‑crash, low‑sentiment play that could rebound sharply if a feasibility study or partnership materialises, offering a high‑reward, high‑risk contrarian opportunity.
